Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search emails for Raymond Chen's QC findings using search_emails (query: "QC" or "Tyler" or "conditions cleared" or "spot check" or similar). The agent will discover Raymond's email to Grace (subject: "QC exceptions found on 3 Tyler-cleared files") identifying three loans, LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224, all with conditions cleared by Tyler Washington without supporting documents in the file. The email describes LN-2026-00603 as closed and the other two as open.
- Search the LOS for each QC-affected loan using mortgage_los_search_loans, mortgage_los_get_loan (by loan_number), or mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id to retrieve statuses, assigned loan officers, and borrower information. The agent will discover that LN-2026-00224 is closed (closing_date 2026-03-10) with Marcus Webb as the assigned LO, connecting the QC thread to the commission leak thread ("someone else who's already on my radar"). The other loans have statuses of processing (LN-2026-00603) and withdrawn (LN-2026-00211). The agent may also use mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: each affected loan's internal ID) to check for specific condition records; these may return empty results, in which case the conditions context comes from Raymond's email narrative.
- Search Slack for internal context about Tyler Washington and the QC findings using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Tyler" or "conditions" or "QC" or "cleared" or similar). The agent will find messages showing Tyler flagged workload strain, and Elena advising to "keep the file moving and get docs in after," providing context on whether he was cutting corners on his own or being pushed to rush.
- Search Stripe for Marcus Webb's post-departure commission transfers using stripe_list_connected_accounts to identify Marcus Webb's connected account (acct_scenario_644_departed, "Marcus Webb Consulting"), then stripe_list_transfers (destination_account_id: "acct_scenario_644_departed") to retrieve the transfer records. The agent will discover five transfers, all dated 2026-04-10, totaling approximately $7,655: $1,250 on LN-2026-00525, $1,875 on LN-2026-00527, $980 on LN-2026-00008, $1,450 on LN-2026-00211, and $2,100 on LN-2026-00608.
- Search the LOS for Carlos Rivera's loan pipeline using mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: "los_staff_a7fa5b29babd") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Carlos Rivera" or by individual loan numbers). The agent will discover his active and inactive loan files with their current statuses, revealing the production issues Grace already suspects. The agent should identify which files are at immediate risk (e.g., stalled in processing with expired or expiring rate locks such as LN-2026-00184 and LN-2026-00620) and flag those specifically.
- Search Slack and/or email for internal discussions about Carlos Rivera using conversations_search_messages or search_emails (query: "Carlos" or "Rivera" or "production" or "pipeline" or similar). The agent will find messages from referral partners and processors discussing his production drought, missed locks, and pipeline concerns, corroborating Grace's assessment.
- Send an email (via send_email) to Denise Holloway (denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) with CC to Raymond Chen (raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com) providing the complete QC investigation findings: the three affected loans, their statuses, assigned LOs, conditions that were cleared without docs, and the compliance/investor exposure concern for the closed file.
- Send an email (via send_email) to Hana Kim (hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com) with the full Marcus Webb post-departure transfer breakdown, individual amounts, associated loan files, total exposure, and an explicit request to freeze those payouts immediately.
- Send a message (via send_email or conversations_add_message) to Raj Anand (raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com) requesting that Marcus Webb's payment account be deactivated from the system.
- Send a consolidated briefing email (via send_email) to Robert Calloway (r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com) covering all three situations in one place: the QC gap (Tyler clearing conditions without docs, affected loans, closed file exposure), the commission leak (Marcus Webb post-departure transfers and amounts), and the Carlos Rivera pipeline status.
- Post a factual message (via conversations_add_message) to #compliance-alerts (channel C004) about the QC irregularities, conditions cleared without supporting documentation, without naming Tyler Washington or other individuals.
- Post a reminder message (via conversations_add_message) to #loan-processing (channel C002) reinforcing that conditions cannot be cleared without verified supporting docs in the file.
- Send a communication (via send_email or conversations_add_message) to Elena Marchetti (elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com) identifying which QC-affected files are still open so she can begin document remediation.
- Add activity notes (via mortgage_los_add_activity) to each QC-affected loan file in the LOS flagging them as under compliance review.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
- Search emails for Raymond Chen's QC findings using search_emails (query: "QC" or "Tyler" or "conditions cleared" or "spot check" or similar). The agent will discover Raymond's email to Grace (subject: "QC exceptions found on 3 Tyler-cleared files") identifying three loans, LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224, all with conditions cleared by Tyler Washington without supporting documents in the file. The email describes LN-2026-00603 as closed and the other two as open.
- Search the LOS for each QC-affected loan using mortgage_los_search_loans, mortgage_los_get_loan (by loan_number), or mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id to retrieve statuses, assigned loan officers, and borrower information. The agent will discover that LN-2026-00224 is closed (closing_date 2026-03-10) with Marcus Webb as the assigned LO, connecting the QC thread to the commission leak thread ("someone else who's already on my radar"). The other loans have statuses of processing (LN-2026-00603) and withdrawn (LN-2026-00211). The agent may also use mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: each affected loan's internal ID) to check for specific condition records; these may return empty results, in which case the conditions context comes from Raymond's email narrative.
- Search Slack for internal context about Tyler Washington and the QC findings using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Tyler" or "conditions" or "QC" or "cleared" or similar). The agent will find messages showing Tyler flagged workload strain, and Elena advising to "keep the file moving and get docs in after," providing context on whether he was cutting corners on his own or being pushed to rush.
- Search Stripe for Marcus Webb's post-departure commission transfers using stripe_list_connected_accounts to identify Marcus Webb's connected account (acct_scenario_644_departed, "Marcus Webb Consulting"), then stripe_list_transfers (destination_account_id: "acct_scenario_644_departed") to retrieve the transfer records. The agent will discover five transfers, all dated 2026-04-10, totaling approximately $7,655: $1,250 on LN-2026-00525, $1,875 on LN-2026-00527, $980 on LN-2026-00008, $1,450 on LN-2026-00211, and $2,100 on LN-2026-00608.
- Search the LOS for Carlos Rivera's loan pipeline using mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: "los_staff_a7fa5b29babd") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Carlos Rivera" or by individual loan numbers). The agent will discover his active and inactive loan files with their current statuses, revealing the production issues Grace already suspects. The agent should identify which files are at immediate risk (e.g., stalled in processing with expired or expiring rate locks such as LN-2026-00184 and LN-2026-00620) and flag those specifically.
- Search Slack and/or email for internal discussions about Carlos Rivera using conversations_search_messages or search_emails (query: "Carlos" or "Rivera" or "production" or "pipeline" or similar). The agent will find messages from referral partners and processors discussing his production drought, missed locks, and pipeline concerns, corroborating Grace's assessment.
- Send an email (via send_email) to Denise Holloway (denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com) with CC to Raymond Chen (raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com) providing the complete QC investigation findings: the three affected loans, their statuses, assigned LOs, conditions that were cleared without docs, and the compliance/investor exposure concern for the closed file.
- Send an email (via send_email) to Hana Kim (hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com) with the full Marcus Webb post-departure transfer breakdown, individual amounts, associated loan files, total exposure, and an explicit request to freeze those payouts immediately.
- Send a message (via send_email or conversations_add_message) to Raj Anand (raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com) requesting that Marcus Webb's payment account be deactivated from the system.
- Send a consolidated briefing email (via send_email) to Robert Calloway (r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com) covering all three situations in one place: the QC gap (Tyler clearing conditions without docs, affected loans, closed file exposure), the commission leak (Marcus Webb post-departure transfers and amounts), and the Carlos Rivera pipeline status.
- Post a factual message (via conversations_add_message) to #compliance-alerts (channel C004) about the QC irregularities, conditions cleared without supporting documentation, without naming Tyler Washington or other individuals.
- Post a reminder message (via conversations_add_message) to #loan-processing (channel C002) reinforcing that conditions cannot be cleared without verified supporting docs in the file.

1 An email was sent (via send_email) to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com with raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com in CC.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The trajectory confirms a send_email tool call with recipients: denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and cc: raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com, returning email_id ad44fe5479384d20b98b54da8cb3ee64 (success).
✓Run #3Tool call index 146 shows send_email succeeded to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com with raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com in CC.
✓Run #4Items 132-133 confirm send_email was called with to: denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and CC: raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com. The call succeeded with email_id '32be26beaabb4b7dbdcb9f27a4241900'.
✓Run #5Item 156 shows a send_email call to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com with raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com in CC, and the tool returned success (Email ID: 406546ca84504cddac400f38136ef7f6).
✓Run #6Email 1 (index 136-137) was sent to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com with raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com in CC. Tool returned success with email_id 3d0cb6dd70464a3abfcca3b318d901c2.
2 The email body (in the send_email parameters) sent to Denise Holloway mentions QC-affected loan LN-2026-00603.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✗✓ 67%
✓Run #1The Denise email explicitly lists 'LN-2026-00603 -- Janet Torres (CLOSED FILE -- HIGHEST RISK)' as the first affected file.
✓Run #2LN-2026-00603 is the closed Torres file that the agent identified as one of the three QC-affected loans. The email to Denise was described as a 'Full detailed breakdown of all 3 QC files' and the agent explicitly searched and retrieved LN-2026-00603 from the LOS, making its inclusion in the email highly confirmed.
✓Run #3The Denise email body explicitly lists 'FILE 2: LN-2026-00603' with full detail on borrower, LO, and document gaps.
✗Run #4The Denise email (items 132-133) covers LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00620, and LN-2026-00605 as the three QC-affected files. LN-2026-00603 is not mentioned anywhere in the email. The agent identified different loan files than those specified in the criterion.
✗Run #5The email to Denise (Item 156) references LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00008, and LN-2026-00620 as the three QC-affected files. LN-2026-00603 is never mentioned in the email body or anywhere in the trajectory.
✓Run #6The email to Denise explicitly covers the three QC-flagged loans including LN-2026-00603 (Janet Torres, inactive processor, missing URLA/paystub).
3 The email body (in the send_email parameters) sent to Denise Holloway mentions QC-affected loan LN-2026-00211.
outcome
 ✗✗✓✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The agent identified the three QC-affected loans as LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00184, and LN-2026-00623 — not LN-2026-00211. LN-2026-00211 does not appear in the Denise email as a QC file.
✗Run #2The agent identified the three QC-affected files as LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00556, and LN-2026-00184 — not LN-2026-00211. LN-2026-00211 was only associated with Marcus Webb's commission transfers in the trajectory. The email to Denise would not contain LN-2026-00211 as a QC file.
✓Run #3The Denise email body references LN-2026-00211 in the 'ADDITIONAL FLAG — MARCUS WEBB CONNECTION' section: 'including LN-2026-00211.'
✗Run #4The Denise email (items 132-133) covers LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00620, and LN-2026-00605 as the QC-affected files. LN-2026-00211 is referenced only as a Marcus Webb commission transfer target, not as a QC-affected file in the Denise email.
✗Run #5The email to Denise (Item 156) does not mention LN-2026-00211 in the QC context. The agent used different loan numbers (00608, 00008, 00620) for the three QC-affected files. LN-2026-00211 appears only in the Marcus Webb commission breakdown.
✗Run #6The agent identified LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00603, and LN-2026-00556 as the three QC-affected loans. LN-2026-00211 was not identified as a QC-affected file; it appears only as a Stripe transfer destination. The Denise email does not reference LN-2026-00211 as a QC-flagged file.
4 The email body (in the send_email parameters) sent to Denise Holloway mentions QC-affected loan LN-2026-00224.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent never identified LN-2026-00224 as a QC-affected file. The Denise email lists LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00184, and LN-2026-00623 as the three affected files. LN-2026-00224 is absent.
✗Run #2The agent identified the three QC-affected files as LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00556, and LN-2026-00184 — not LN-2026-00224. LN-2026-00224 was never referenced in the agent's trajectory. The email to Denise would not contain LN-2026-00224.
✗Run #3LN-2026-00224 does not appear anywhere in the Denise email body. The agent identified the three QC loans as LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00603, and LN-2026-00556, missing LN-2026-00224 entirely.
✗Run #4The Denise email (items 132-133) covers LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00620, and LN-2026-00605. LN-2026-00224 is not mentioned in the email to Denise at all — the agent identified different loan files as the QC-affected ones.
✗Run #5The email to Denise (Item 156) does not mention LN-2026-00224 at all. The agent identified different loan numbers as the QC-affected files and LN-2026-00224 does not appear anywhere in the trajectory.
✗Run #6The agent identified LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00603, and LN-2026-00556 as the QC-affected loans. LN-2026-00224 was never identified or mentioned in any email, including the one to Denise.
5 The email body (in the send_email parameters) sent to Denise Holloway mentions compliance risk, investor exposure, or post-close liability associated with the closed loan file.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Denise email explicitly references the closed file (LN-2026-00603) as the 'most immediate compliance and repurchase exposure' and asks Denise to assess 'investor repurchase exposure and notification obligation,' covering compliance and investor exposure language.
✓Run #2The trajectory describes the Denise email as including 'investor exposure analysis' for the closed loan (LN-2026-00603 with Flagstar Wholesale). The email subject and body explicitly addressed compliance/investor exposure for the closed file.
✓Run #3The Denise email explicitly states 'This is the file with immediate investor exposure' for the closed loan, and asks Denise to assess 'Investor notification requirements,' 'repurchase risk evaluation,' and 'regulatory disclosure.'
✓Run #4The Denise email (items 132-133) explicitly calls out LN-2026-00608 as closed (Apr 3) and highest risk, and covers investor exposure concerns and compliance assessment. The trajectory summary confirms 'investor exposure concerns, requested actions' in the email content.
✓Run #5The email to Denise (Item 156) explicitly flags LN-2026-00608 as 'CLOSED 4/3/2026 — HIGHEST RISK' and mentions 'investor buyback risk,' directly addressing compliance and investor exposure on the closed file.
✓Run #6The email to Denise explicitly identifies LN-2026-00608 as the closed file with investor exposure to Homepoint Financial due to income doc gaps, requesting compliance assessment including investor notification obligations.
6 The message to Hana Kim (in send_email or conversations_add_message parameters) includes the $1,250 post-departure commission transfer on loan file LN-2026-00525.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Hana email explicitly lists '$1,250.00 -- LN-2026-00525 (Chen file)' as transfer #4 in the post-departure breakdown.
✓Run #2The agent's Hana email was described as 'Itemized all 5 post-departure transfers ($7,655 total).' The agent's final response confirms $1,250 on LN-2026-00525 as one of the five transfers, indicating it was included in the Hana email.
✓Run #3Hana email lists '4. $1,250.00 — LO commission, Chen file (LN-2026-00525)' explicitly.
✓Run #4Items 134-135 confirm the Hana email lists transfer tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn for $1,250 on LN-2026-00525 as one of five post-departure transfers. The email was successfully sent.
✓Run #5The email to Hana Kim (Item 158) explicitly lists LN-2026-00525 (Chen/Vargas): $1,250.00 as one of the post-departure transfers.
✓Run #6Email 2 to Hana Kim lists all 5 post-departure transfers. The Stripe call confirmed tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn for $1,250 on LN-2026-00525 (Webb -- Chen) is included in the breakdown.
7 The message to Hana Kim (in send_email or conversations_add_message parameters) includes the $1,875 post-departure commission transfer on loan file LN-2026-00527.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Hana email explicitly lists '$1,875.00 -- LN-2026-00527 (Patel file)' as transfer #1 in the post-departure breakdown.
✓Run #2The agent's Hana email itemized all 5 post-departure transfers. The final response confirms $1,875 on LN-2026-00527, and the agent specifically searched mortgage_los_search_loans for LN-2026-00527 confirming it was included in the breakdown.

outcome

✓Run #2The trajectory confirms a conversations_add_message to channel C004 (#compliance-alerts) posting an 'anonymized notice about QC irregularities found in audit; reminder that clearing conditions without docs is a compliance violation.' The tool call succeeded.

✓Run #4Items 142-143 confirm conversations_add_message was called with channel_id 'C004' (#compliance-alerts) with content about QC condition clearance irregularities affecting several files including one already closed. The call succeeded.
✓Run #5Item 166 shows a conversations_add_message to channel C004 (#compliance-alerts) with a generic heads-up about QC irregularities without naming individuals, and the tool returned success (ts: 1775931398.784944).
✓Run #6Message 1 (index 146-147) was successfully posted to channel C004 (#compliance-alerts), announcing QC irregularities involving conditions cleared without supporting documentation.
28 The message posted to #compliance-alerts (C004) does NOT include Tyler Washington's name or other individual employee names.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The #compliance-alerts message reads as fully anonymous — it references 'loan files' and 'a QC review' without naming Tyler Washington or any other individual employee. Only role-agnostic language is used.
✓Run #2The trajectory explicitly describes the #compliance-alerts message as 'anonymized notice' with no individual names. The agent's action item was to post with 'no names,' and the trajectory confirms this was done as an anonymized/factual post.
✓Run #3The #compliance-alerts message body contains no individual employee names — no mention of 'Tyler,' 'Tyler Washington,' or any other named Keystone staff member.
✓Run #4Items 142-143 confirm the #compliance-alerts message had 'No names mentioned' per the trajectory summary, complying with the 'no names' instruction.

✗Run #3The agent never identified Marcus Webb as the LO on LN-2026-00224. The agent's cross-connection references Marcus Webb in relation to LN-2026-00211 (undisclosed conflict) and LN-2026-00608 (commission leak), but never identifies him as LO on LN-2026-00224.
✗Run #4The agent did not identify Marcus Webb as the LO on any QC-affected file. The agent's QC investigation pointed to James Thornton and Carlos Rivera as LOs on the affected files. While Marcus Webb's wife (Danielle Webb) was flagged as a borrower on LN-2026-00629, the direct LO connection to LN-2026-00224 was not discovered.
✗Run #5The agent never identified Marcus Webb as the LO on any QC-affected file. The agent's QC findings used loan numbers 00608, 00008, and 00620 — not the expected 00603/00211/00224 where the Marcus Webb connection would have been found. No email or response references this cross-thread connection.
✓Run #6The email to Denise and the agent's final response both note the overlap with Marcus Webb receiving a $2,100 commission on LN-2026-00608 (one of the QC-affected files). The final response explicitly calls out the Marcus Webb connection and cross-thread significance.
35 The agent's communications (email to Denise Holloway, briefing to Robert Calloway, or final response) address whether Tyler Washington was acting independently or under pressure — e.g., referencing Elena Marchetti's advice to keep files moving and upload docs later, Tyler's workload strain, or Carlos Rivera pushing files before lock expiration (or similar contextual factors).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Both the Denise email and Robert email explicitly address whether Tyler acted alone, citing Carlos Rivera's lock-expiration pressure on LN-2026-00184 and Tyler's own written acknowledgment ('I know I messed up...00184 had lock pressure all over it from Carlos'), plus Tyler being overwhelmed with workload.
✓Run #2The agent's final response explicitly addressed Tyler's motivations: 'Tyler acknowledged in Slack he messed up — was overwhelmed with volume and deadline pressure. Critically, he stated Carlos Rivera was pressuring him to rush LN-2026-00184 due to rate lock deadlines.' The Denise email and Robert briefing both covered this context.
✓Run #3Both the Denise and Robert emails reference Tyler's Slack messages about being 'buried in conds + closing stuff and the lock files are all hitting at once' and characterize the clearances as an 'individual shortcut under workload pressure' with no evidence of direction from others.
✓Run #4The agent's final response explicitly addresses whether Tyler acted alone or under pressure: 'Internal Slack shows him and Keisha Williams pressuring about compliance timelines, which likely created an environment for shortcuts. Whether Tyler was directed or acted alone remains to be determined.' This analysis appears in the agent's response which is part of its communications.
✓Run #5The email to Denise (Item 156) explicitly addresses the pressure angle, noting Keisha Williams had docs in her personal email that never hit the LOS and was pushing back against enhanced reviews. The agent also found Slack messages showing this context, addressing whether Tyler was acting independently or under pressure.
✓Run #6The agent's final response explicitly addresses the 'pushing' question: 'Tyler's Slack messages show he was simultaneously overwhelmed on all three files. No direct evidence yet of someone pushing him to rush, but the pattern suggests workload/deadline pressure rather than directive from above.' This finding was reported in the final response and likely the Denise email.
